The Lego Movie (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $258,364,131Details
International Box Office $208,864,876
Worldwide Box Office $467,229,007
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $66,051,192
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $45,284,112
Total $111,335,304
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Emmet is an ordinary, rules-following, perfectly average LEGO mini-figure who is mistakenly identified as the most extraordinary person and the key to saving the world. He is drafted into a fellowship of strangers on an epic quest to stop an evil tyrant, a journey for which Emmet is hopelessly under-prepared.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$69,050,279 (26.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.74 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:55.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 7.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,775 opening theaters/3,890 max. theaters, 8.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $377,586,218

Movie Details

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Emmet is an ordinary, rules-following, perfectly average LEGO mini-figure who is mistakenly identified as the most extraordinary person and the key to saving the world. He is drafted into a fellowship of strangers on an epic quest to stop an evil tyrant, a journey for which Emmet is hopelessly under-prepared.
